growth_records.medium: Complex medium + 20 mM fructose (bicarbonate-buffered, +Na+), stirred-tank reactor (STR)

up.method: not calculable (biomass in gDW/L not reported, only OD600)

sec.units: mol acetate/mol fructose (yield, not gDW-normalized rate)
mmol H2/mol fructose (yield, not gDW-normalized rate)

growth_records.cite: Wiechmann et al. It does not always take two to tango: "Syntrophy" via hydrogen cycling in one bacterial cell. The ISME Journal (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-0627-1. [PMC7242416]
growth_records.snippet: "Cultures reached a maximum OD600 of 2.28 after 24 h ... the growth rate was 0.19 h-1 ... 2.4 mol of acetic acid and 8.4+/-0.2 mmol of hydrogen were produced per mol of fructose consumed." (Table 2, WT+Na+)
growth_records.notes: mu directly reported in Table 2 (STR-cultivation, WT +Na+). Product/substrate yields (mol/mol, mmol/mol) reported directly in Table 2 but NOT converted to specific mmol/gDW/h rates because biomass concentration in gDW/L is not given (only OD600); listed as yields, not rates, to avoid inventing an OD-to-gDW conversion factor not stated in the paper.

growth_records.medium: Complex medium + 20 mM fructose, Na+-depleted, stirred-tank reactor (STR)
growth_records.cite: Wiechmann et al. It does not always take two to tango: "Syntrophy" via hydrogen cycling in one bacterial cell. The ISME Journal (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-0627-1. [PMC7242416]
growth_records.snippet: Table 2: WT -Na+, mu=0.11 h-1, yield acetate 2.01 mol/mol, yield H2 18.74 mmol/mol.
growth_records.notes: mu and yields directly reported in Table 2 (STR-cultivation, WT, Na+-depleted complex medium).

growth_records.medium: Complex medium + 20 mM fructose (Na+ status not explicitly labeled for this column; inferred low/no added Na+ from surrounding text), stirred-tank reactor (STR)
growth_records.cite: Wiechmann et al. It does not always take two to tango: "Syntrophy" via hydrogen cycling in one bacterial cell. The ISME Journal (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-0627-1. [PMC7242416]
growth_records.snippet: Table 2: rnf mutant, mu=0.13 h-1, yield acetate 2.06 mol/mol, yield H2 17.81 mmol/mol.
growth_records.notes: mu and yields directly reported in Table 2. Na+ condition for this column is ambiguous in the source text ('Similar results were observed for the rnf deletion mutant' following a discussion of decreasing Na+); flagged medium/confidence accordingly.

growth_records.medium: Bicarbonate-buffered complex medium + 20 mM fructose, under H2+CO2 (80:20 v/v) headspace atmosphere

sec.units: mol acetate/mol fructose (approx yield, stationary phase)

growth_records.cite: Wiechmann et al. It does not always take two to tango: "Syntrophy" via hydrogen cycling in one bacterial cell. The ISME Journal (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-0627-1. [PMC7242416]
growth_records.snippet: "The growth rate of wild type and mutant (Fig. 3a) was similar (each ~0.16 h-1) as well as the acetate:fructose ratio of 3:1 in the stationary phase (Fig. 3b)"
growth_records.notes: mu (~0.16 h-1) and acetate:fructose ratio (~3:1) directly reported for both wild type and delta-hydBA mutant under fructose + H2/CO2.

growth_records.medium: Complex medium + 20 mM fructose + 100 mM formate (+CO2)
growth_records.cite: Wiechmann et al. It does not always take two to tango: "Syntrophy" via hydrogen cycling in one bacterial cell. The ISME Journal (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-0627-1. [PMC7242416]
growth_records.snippet: "the hydBA mutant grew with a doubling time of ~5 h on 20 mM fructose and 100 mM formate, which is slightly slower than the growth of the wild type (~3.5 h-4 h)"
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h = ln(2)/doubling_time_h using the paper-reported doubling time of ~5 h.

growth_records.medium: Complex medium + 20 mM fructose + 100 mM formate (+CO2)
growth_records.cite: Wiechmann et al. It does not always take two to tango: "Syntrophy" via hydrogen cycling in one bacterial cell. The ISME Journal (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-0627-1. [PMC7242416]
growth_records.snippet: "...slightly slower than the growth of the wild type (~3.5 h-4 h)"
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h = ln(2)/doubling_time_h using the midpoint (3.75 h) of the paper-reported range '~3.5 h-4 h' for wild-type doubling time; range itself not a single point value so a midpoint was used and flagged.

growth_records.medium: Complex medium + 20 mM fructose + 6 mM caffeate
growth_records.cite: Wiechmann et al. It does not always take two to tango: "Syntrophy" via hydrogen cycling in one bacterial cell. The ISME Journal (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-0627-1. [PMC7242416]
growth_records.snippet: "Doubling times of the mutant and the wild type were similar (~6 h), but growth of the mutant culture stopped much earlier, with a final OD600 of 0.68 compared with 3.2 of the wild type."
growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h = ln(2)/doubling_time_h using the paper-reported doubling time of ~6 h (reported as similar for WT and mutant).

growth_records.medium: Autotrophic H2/CO2 gas mixture (modeling; experimental reference values from Novak et al. 2021)

up.method: maximal H2 uptake rate used as model bound; cited as within range of maximal H2 uptake reported by Novak et al. 2021 (ref 21)
specific CO2 uptake rate; cited from Table 4 of Novak et al. 2021 (ref 21)

sec.method: primary WLP fermentation product; model reproduced measured acetate excretion within <3% (Supplementary Table 3) vs Novak et al./Neuendorf et al. data

mfa.reaction_or_metabolite: H2 uptake / CO2 uptake / acetate excretion (WLP)
mfa.note: Model flux distributions matched measured growth, substrate uptake and product excretion rates in almost all cases (Supplementary Table 3); only minor <3% adjustment to measured CO2 uptake and acetate excretion needed for consistency; formate-substrate data showed carbon imbalance from yeast extract (ref 26)

growth_records.cite: Bauer J et al. Thermodynamic metabolic modeling of growth and bioproduction potential of the acetogen Acetobacterium woodii with and without redox cofactor swaps. Synthetic and Systems Biotechnology (2026). DOI:10.1016/j.synbio.2026.05.007. [PMC13279740]
growth_records.snippet: we limited the specific uptake rate of H2 to 120 mmol gDW-1 h-1 (which is in the range of maximal H2 uptake rates reported by Novak et al. [21]) and of CO2 to 60 ... a specific CO2 uptake rate of 39.7 mmol gDW-1 h-1 (table 4 in Ref. [21]) .
growth_records.notes: MODELING paper - reports NO new experimental rate; all experimental values are CITED. Measured A. woodii growth on H2/CO2 typically ~0.1 h-1 (refs 21 Novak et al 2021 Bioresour Technol 323, and 22); model mu_max=0.107 h-1. Experimental H2 uptake max ~120 mmol/gDW/h and CO2 uptake 39.7 mmol/gDW/h cited from Novak et al. 2021 (ref 21, Table 4). Model validated against measured growth/uptake/excretion in Supplementary Table 3 (data from Novak et al ref 21 and Neuendorf et al ref 26 Metab Eng 68:68-85). T=298.15 K (25C), cytosolic pH 7.5 used in thermodynamic calcs. td=ln2/0.1=6.93 h. Included for reference; rates are literature-cited, not measured herein.
